files.Title: CXEX.CLI.Commands.CompileCommand
files.Summary: Compiles an X source file (.x) to an ELF, ready for cxk build to package as CXEX. Pipeline: [abi.x prelude] + source -> Lexer -> Parser -> Resolver -> TypeChecker -> X86Emitter (-> .s) -> i686-elf-gcc assemble -> link @0x400000.

files.Title: CXEX.Disk.DiskAnalyzer
files.Summary: Entry point: sniff a disk image's partition table and parse it. Reads only the first sectors (plus the GPT entry array) - never loads the whole image. Detection order: GPT ("EFI PART" @ LBA1) -> XBPT ("XBPT" @ LBA1) -> MBR (0x55AA @ 0x1FE).

files.Title: CXEX.Disk.Models.DiskLayout
files.Summary: Parsed view of a disk image: which table, and its partitions.

files.Title: CXEX.Disk.Models.PartitionEntry
files.Summary: One partition / region within a disk image.

files.Title: CXEX.Disk.Parsers.GptParser
files.Summary: Parses a GPT header (LBA1) + its partition entry array.

files.Title: CXEX.Disk.Parsers.MbrParser
files.Summary: Parses a classic MBR partition table (4 primary entries @ 0x1BE).

files.Title: CXEX.Disk.Parsers.XbptParser
files.Summary: XBPT (X Boot Partition Table) reader. Matches CXEX.Build.XBPTImageWriter. Header @ LBA1: magic "XBPT" (u32), version (u16@4), count (u16@6), entrySize (u16@8, =32), totalSectors (u64@12). Entries from @32, 32 bytes: StartLba u64, SectorCount u64, TypeCode u8@16, Flags u8@17, Label ASCII@20[12].

files.Title: CXEX.Lang.Abi.AbiPrelude
files.Summary: Generates the X prelude (abi.x) from the frozen CXK ABI (abi/cxk_abi.h): the SYS_* numbers, E_* error codes, the shared arg structs, and typed __syscall wrappers. Compiling against this keeps X programs in lockstep with the kernel - one source of truth across kernel, C, .NET, and X. v0.1 emits the frozen v1 values directly; a later version can parse cxk_abi.h to stay automatic.

files.Title: CXEX.Lang.Ast.TypeRef
files.Summary: Static type references as written in source (resolved/checked in Sema).

files.Title: CXEX.Lang.Diagnostics.DiagnosticBag
files.Summary: Collects diagnostics across a compile. HasErrors gates later phases.

files.Title: CXEX.Lang.Diagnostics.SourceSpan
files.Summary: A half-open [Start,End) byte range in a source file, with line/col for messages.

files.Title: CXEX.Lang.Lexer.Lexer
files.Summary: X core v0.1 lexer. Single forward pass over UTF-8 source text, tracking line/col for spans. Skips whitespace and // line + /* block */ comments. Reports lexical errors into the bag but keeps going (emits an Error token and advances) so the parser can recover and surface multiple diagnostics.

files.Title: CXEX.Lang.Lexer.Token
files.Summary: A lexed token. Text is the exact source slice; for IntLiteral, Value holds the parsed number.

files.Title: CXEX.Lang.Parsing.Parser
files.Summary: Recursive-descent parser for X core v0.1. Expressions use precedence climbing. On error it reports a diagnostic and synchronizes to the next ';' or '}' / top- level keyword, so one bad construct doesn't cascade. Returns a CompilationUnit regardless; callers check diag.HasErrors before proceeding to Sema.

files.Title: CXEX.Lang.Sema.ConstFold
files.Summary: Compile-time constant evaluation for X core v0.1: integer/bool literals, the arithmetic/comparison/logical operators, unary neg/not, casts (value-preserving), and references to other consts. Used for const initializers, global initializers, and array lengths. Returns false (with a diagnostic) for anything not constant.

files.Title: CXEX.Lang.Sema.Resolver
files.Summary: Pass 1: collect top-level declarations, then resolve every name to a symbol, tracking lexical scopes for params/locals. Reports duplicates + undefined names.

files.Title: CXEX.Lang.Sema.Scope
files.Summary: Lexical scope chain. Globals live at the root; functions push a child.

files.Title: CXEX.Lang.Sema.SemaContext
files.Summary: Shared semantic state produced by the Resolver and consumed by the TypeChecker: top-level symbols, struct decls by name, and per-NameExpr resolution.

files.Title: CXEX.Lang.Sema.TypeChecker
files.Summary: Pass 2: assign a type to every expression and check statements. v0.1 is deliberately lenient on integer width (any int coerces to any int) so the language is usable before a full numeric-conversion policy exists; pointers, bools, structs, and arrays are checked strictly. Records each expr's type in ctx.Types for CodeGen. The __syscall intrinsic is the one effect primitive.

files.Title: CXEX.SDK.ViewLocator
files.Summary: Given a view model, returns the corresponding view if possible.
